questions.question_markdown: Let $\left(\mathbb{Z}_{10},+, \cdot\right)$ be the ring of integers modulo 10 , and let $S$ be the subset of $\mathbb{Z}_{10}$ represented by $\{0,2,4,6,8\}$. Which of the following statements is FALSE?

( $S,+, \cdot$ ) has no identity under multiplication modulo 10 .

questions.question_markdown:
Consider the system of linear equations

$$
\begin{aligned}w+3 x+2 y+2 z & =0 \\ w+4 x+y & =0 \\ 3 w+5 x+10 y+14 z & =0 \\ 2 w+5 x+5 y+6 z & =0\end{aligned}
$$

with solutions of the form $(w, x, y, z)$, where $w, x, y$, and $z$ are real. Which of the following statements is FALSE?
options: The system has infinitely many solutions.
The sum of any two solutions is a solution.
Every solution is a scalar multiple of $(-5,1,1,0)$.
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: E. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown: Suppose $S$ is a nonempty subset of $\mathbb{R}$. Which of the following is necessarily true?
options: For each $s, t \in S$, there exists a continuous function $f$ mapping [ 0,1 ] into $S$ with $f(0)=s$ and $f(1)=t$.
For each $u \notin S$, there exists an open subset $U$ of $\mathbb{R}$ such that $u \in U$ and $U \cap S=\varnothing$.
$\{v \in S$ : there exists an open subset $V$ of $\mathbb{R}$ with $v \in V \subseteq S\}$ is an open subset of $\mathbb{R}$.
$\{w \notin S$ : there exists an open subset $W$ of $\mathbb{R}$ with $w \in W$ and $W \cap S=\varnothing\}$ is a closed subset of $\mathbb{R}$.
$S$ is the intersection of all closed subsets of $\mathbb{R}$ that contain $S$.
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: C. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

III. $P$ is either the identity transformation or the zero transformation.

questions.question_markdown:
Consider the following algorithm, which takes an input integer $\mathrm{n}>2$ and prints one or more integers.

```
input(n)
set $i=1$
while i<n
    begin
        replace i by i+1
        set $k=n$
        while k $\geq$ i
    begin
            if i=k then print(i)
        replace k by $\mathrm{k}-1$
    end
    end
```

If the input integer is 88 , what integers will be printed?
options: Only the divisors of 88 that are greater than 1
The integers from 2 to 88 in increasing order
The integers from 88 to 2 in decreasing order
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: D. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown:
Let $S$ be the set of all functions $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$. Consider the two binary operations + and o on $S$ defined as pointwise addition and composition of functions, as follows.

$$
\begin{aligned}(f+g)(x) & =f(x)+g(x) \\ (f \circ g)(x) & =f(g(x))\end{aligned}
$$

Which of the following statements are true?

I. ∘ is commutative.

II. + and ∘ satisfy the left distributive law $f \circ(g+h)=(f \circ g)+(f \circ h)$.

III. + and ∘ satisfy the right distributive law $(g+h) \circ f=(g \circ f)+(h \circ f)$.
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: C. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown:
Let $\mathbb{Z}^{+}$be the set of positive integers and let $d$ be the metric on $\mathbb{Z}^{+}$defined by

$$
d(m, n)= \begin{cases}0 & \text { if } m=n

 1 & \text { if } m \neq n\end{cases}
$$

for all $m, n \in \mathbb{Z}^{+}$. Which of the following statements are true about the metric space $\left(\mathbb{Z}^{+}, d\right)$ ?

I. If $n \in \mathbb{Z}^{+}$, then $\{n\}$ is an open subset of $\mathbb{Z}^{+}$.

II. Every subset of $\mathbb{Z}^{+}$is closed.

III. Every real-valued function defined on $\mathbb{Z}^{+}$is continuous.
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: E. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown: A ladder 9 meters in length is leaning against a vertical wall on level ground. As the bottom end of the ladder is moved away from the wall at a constant rate of 2 meters per second, the top end slides downward along the wall. questions.question_markdown:
The function $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ is defined as follows.

$$
f(x)=\left\{\begin{aligned}3 x^{2} & \text { if } x \in \mathbb{Q} \\ -5 x^{2} & \text { if } x \notin \mathbb{Q}\end{aligned}\right.
$$

Which of the following is true?
options: $f$ is continuous only at $x=0$ and differentiable only at $x=0$.
$f$ is continuous only at $x=0$ and nondifferentiable at all $x \in \mathbb{R}$.
$f$ is continuous at all $x \in \mathbb{Q}$ and nondifferentiable at all $x \in \mathbb{R}$.
$f$ is continuous at all $x \notin \mathbb{Q}$ and nondifferentiable at all $x \in \mathbb{R}$.
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: B. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown:
Which of the following statements are true?

I. There exists a constant $C$ such that $\log x \leq C \sqrt{x}$ for all $x \geq 1$.

II. There exists a constant $C$ such that $\sum_{k=1}^{n} k^{2} \leq C n^{2}$ for all integers $n \geq 1$.

I. $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} x_{n}=0$

II. If $f$ is a continuous real-valued function defined on $(0,1)$, then $\left\{f\left(x_{n}\right)\right\}_{n=1}^{\infty}$ is a Cauchy sequence.

III. If $g$ is a uniformly continuous real-valued function defined on $(0,1)$, then $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} g\left(x_{n}\right)$ exists.

questions.question_markdown: Let $A$ be a real $3 \times 3$ matrix. Which of the following conditions does NOT imply that $A$ is invertible?
options: There exists a positive integer $k$ such that $\det\left(A^{k}\right) \neq 0$.
There exists a positive integer $k$ such that $(I-A)^{k}=0$, where $I$ is the $3 \times 3$ identity matrix.
The set of all vectors of the form $A \mathbf{v}$, where $\mathbf{v} \in \mathbb{R}^{3}$, is $\mathbb{R}^{3}$.
There exist 3 linearly independent vectors $\mathbf{v}_{1}, \mathbf{v}_{2}, \mathbf{v}_{3} \in \mathbb{R}^{3}$ such that $A \mathbf{v}_{i} \neq \mathbf{0}$ for each $i$.
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: E. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.

questions.question_markdown: A tank initially contains a salt solution of 3 grams of salt dissolved in 100 liters of water. If the mixing is instantaneous, how many grams of salt are in the tank after 100 minutes have elapsed?

questions.question_markdown: Let $S$ be the subset of $\mathbb{R}^{2}$ consisting of all points $(x, y)$ in the unit square $[0,1] \times[0,1]$ for which $x$ or $y$, or both, are irrational. With respect to the standard topology on $\mathbb{R}^{2}, S$ is
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: C. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown:
For any nonempty sets $A$ and $B$ of real numbers, let $A \cdot B$ be the set defined by

$$
A \cdot B=\{x y: x \in A \text { and } y \in B\} .
$$

If $A$ and $B$ are nonempty bounded sets of real numbers and if $\sup (A)>\sup (B)$, then $\sup (A \cdot B)=$
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: E. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown: What is the value of the flux of the vector field $\mathbf{F}$, defined on $\mathbb{R}^{3}$ by $\mathbf{F}(x, y, z)=x \mathbf{i}+y \mathbf{j}+z \mathbf{k}$, through the surface $z=\sqrt{1-x^{2}-y^{2}}$ oriented with upward-pointing normal vector field? (Note: $\mathbf{i}, \mathbf{j}$, and $\mathbf{k}$ are the standard basis vectors in $\mathbb{R}^{3}$.)
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: E. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown:
Let $g$ be a differentiable function of two real variables, and let $f$ be the function of a complex variable $z$ defined by

$$
f(z)=e^{x} \sin y+i g(x, y)
$$

where $x$ and $y$ are the real and imaginary parts of $z$, respectively. If $f$ is an analytic function on the complex plane, then $g(3,2)-g(1,2)=$
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: E. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268

questions.question_markdown:
Let $\mathbb{Z}_{17}$ be the ring of integers modulo 17 , and let $\mathbb{Z}_{17}{ }^{\times}$be the group of units of $\mathbb{Z}_{17}$ under multiplication. Which of the following are generators of $\mathbb{Z}_{17}{ }^{\times}$?

I. 5

II. 8

III. 16
questions.solution_markdown: Official answer key: B. Worked solutions are not included in the source practice book.
questions.source_note: ETS GRE Mathematics Test Practice Book, Form GR1268
